finding: The committed deployment proof builder emits schema_version 1.1.0 with url_authority, while the prior validator accepted only 1.0.0. This created a deterministic contract failure before any live deployment proof could be accepted.

behavior: accepts 1.1.0 only when url_authority is present and valid
requires authority deployment and alias origins to match the proof URLs

verification.method: isolated Node.js runtime using byte-identical validator logic and equivalent committed test fixtures
verification.limits: The full GitHub Actions workflow was not executed because Cloudflare environment credentials remain externally unconfigured. The workflow still needs to invoke build-deployment-proof.mjs and include this new test in its contract suite.

privacy_boundary: No credentials, account identifiers, private chat, health material, diagnosis, or treatment claims are included.
falsification_route: Run the committed test in GitHub Actions.
Construct a 1.1.0 proof with missing or mismatched url_authority and require rejection.
Run a credentialed deployment and require the emitted proof to pass both URL policy and proof validation.
Attempt an unknown schema version and require fail-closed behavior.
